steps.title: Spot the contrast word
steps.content: The word however signals a contrast between two independent clauses: the rarity of retroflex consonants in many languages and their relative commonness in Mandarin dialects.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: To join two independent clauses with a conjunctive adverb like however, the SAT requires a semicolon before it and a comma after: IC; however, IC. Placing a semicolon after the blank gives rare; producing the correct pattern: these consonants are rare; in some dialects of Mandarin, however, four such consonants ... are relatively common. The other choices either insert a comma before however (creating a comma splice), leave no punctuation (also a comma splice), or use and (ungrammatical). That matches choice C — C is correct.
